home.title: Tools
home.subtitle: Choose a tool to get started.

follower.title: Follower of followers
follower.description: Automatically follow the followers of a target profile, with configurable filters, rate limits and humanized delays.

unfollower.title: Follower remover
unfollower.description: Automatically unfollow accounts that don't follow you back, with filters to protect specific profiles.

postManager.title: Post manager
postManager.description: Archive everything, bulk delete, filter by post type (photos, reels, carousels) and manage your profile efficiently.

feedback.title: Send feedback
feedback.subtitle: Suggestions, bugs or compliments — all are welcome.

feedback.successBody: We'll review your feedback as soon as possible.
feedback.error: Failed to send. Please try again.

nav.title: Instagram Controller
nav.betaWarning: This tool is in beta. Use it sparingly and at your own risk — misuse may result in temporary restrictions on your Instagram account.

form.title: Configuration
form.description: Configure your credentials and follow mode.

sessionId.label: Session ID
sessionId.tooltip: Authentication token for your Instagram session. To get it, open Instagram in your browser, open DevTools (F12), go to Application → Cookies and copy the value of the sessionid cookie.

targetUser.label: Target user
targetUser.tooltip: The profile whose followers you want to follow. The bot will iterate through that user's follower list and follow each one according to the configured filters.

mode.label: Follow mode
mode.tooltip: Defines the follow speed. Faster modes increase the risk of temporary blocking by the platform. We recommend Safe mode for continuous use.

filters.label: Account filters
filters.alreadyFollowers: Follow accounts that already follow me
filters.minFollowers: Follow only accounts with a minimum number of followers

progress.title: Following {'@'}{user}
progress.keepTabOpen: Do not close this tab while the process is running.

form.title: Configuration
form.description: Configure your credentials and removal mode.

mode.label: Removal mode
mode.tooltip: Defines the removal speed. Faster modes increase the risk of temporary blocking by the platform. We recommend Safe mode for continuous use.

filters.label: Filters
filters.notFollowingBack: Remove only accounts that don't follow me back
filters.minFollowers: Skip accounts with fewer than X followers
filters.maxFollowers: Skip accounts with more than X followers

progress.title: Removing followings
progress.keepTabOpen: Do not close this tab while the process is running.

aboutUnfollow.title: How it works
aboutUnfollow.subtitle: Understand the security mechanisms, available filters, and risks of each removal mode.

overview.title: Overview
overview.p1: The tool accesses your own account's following list and, for each account found, applies the configured filters. If the account passes all filters, the unfollow is executed with a random delay between each action to simulate human behavior.
overview.p2: All processing happens on the local server — no data is sent to external services other than Instagram itself. The session is authenticated with your Session ID, which never leaves the local environment.

notFollowingBack.title: Remove only accounts that don't follow me back
notFollowingBack.body: For each account in the list, the app queries the friendship status via API before executing the unfollow. If the account follows you back (followed_by = true), it is skipped. Note: this filter generates an extra API call per account, which increases total execution time and request volume.

minFollowers.title: Skip accounts with fewer than X followers
minFollowers.body: Before executing the unfollow, the app fetches the account's profile to check the follower count. Accounts with fewer followers than the configured value are skipped. Useful for preserving followings of smaller, closer accounts.

maxFollowers.title: Skip accounts with more than X followers
maxFollowers.body: Similar to the minimum filter, but in the opposite direction. Large accounts (influencers, brands) are preserved. Useful for keeping followings of interest without removing them in bulk.

combining.title: Combining filters
combining.body: Filters are cumulative — an account is only removed if it passes all active filters simultaneously. The more active filters, the more API calls are made per account and the slower the processing. Use sparingly.

delays.title: Delays between removals
delays.body: After each successful unfollow, the app waits between 60 and 120 seconds before the next action. When checking friendship status or fetching profiles, extra delays of 5 to 15 seconds are added per query. These random intervals avoid regular patterns that detection systems easily identify.

batch.title: Batch pauses
batch.body: Every 10 consecutive removals, the app takes an extra break of 2 to 5 minutes. This reproduces the behavior of someone stopping to check the feed, reply to messages, or simply rest between cleanup sessions.

rateLimit.title: Sliding window rate limit
rateLimit.body: Hourly and daily limits are enforced with a real sliding window — not a simple counter that resets at midnight. The day's history is saved in the browser and loaded in the next session, preventing closing and reopening the app from resetting the counter.

retry.title: Retry with backoff on API errors
retry.body: For critical calls, the app retries on HTTP 429 or 503 errors, waiting 30s, 60s, and 90s between attempts. For filter checks, the strategy is to fail fast — if the API doesn't respond, the account is let through instead of freezing the process.

modes.title: Removal modes and risk
modes.intro: Modes control how many removals the app executes per hour and per day. Instagram monitors mass unfollow behavior similarly to follows — high volumes trigger the same defense systems.

ultraSafe.label: Ultra Safe
ultraSafe.body: Very low volume, well below Instagram's internal limits. Suitable for new accounts or those that have already experienced restrictions. With current delays, this limit will rarely be reached.

safe.label: Safe
safe.body: Balance between speed and safety. Recommended for continuous use on accounts with more than 6 months of regular activity. This is the default mode and most suitable for long sessions.

risky.label: Risky
risky.body: Volume close to Instagram's internal limits. May trigger temporary action blocks, reach reduction (shadowban), or in repeated cases, account suspension. Use only on established accounts and occasionally.

consequences.title: What can happen if limits are exceeded

actionBlocked.label: Action Blocked
actionBlocked.body: Instagram temporarily blocks the unfollow action, usually for 24 to 48 hours. The account continues to work normally for other actions. It's the most common warning and generally passes without consequences if behavior is corrected immediately.

shadowban.label: Shadowban
shadowban.body: The account's content stops appearing in hashtags and the Explore tab without notice. The profile continues to exist and post normally, but organic reach drops dramatically. It can last days or weeks.

suspension.label: Temporary or permanent suspension
suspension.body: In cases of abusive and repeated use, Instagram may suspend the account temporarily or permanently. This rarely happens from isolated unfollow automation, but the risk exists especially for new accounts or those with a history of violations.

bestPractices.title: Best practices
items: Use Safe mode as the default. Increase only in specific situations on established accounts.
Do not run the app 24 hours a day. Run periodic cleanup sessions with periods of inactivity in between.
If you receive an "Action Blocked", stop immediately and wait at least 24 hours before resuming.
Avoid combining too many filters simultaneously if you're in a hurry — each filter adds extra API calls and increases total processing time.
The "doesn't follow me back" filter is the most API-intensive. On accounts with many followings, the process can be quite slow.
The Session ID expires when you log out in the browser. Generate a new one whenever the app reports an authentication error.

about.title: How it works
about.subtitle: Understand the security mechanisms, configured limits, and risks of each operation mode.

overview.title: Overview
overview.p1: The app iterates through the followers list of a target profile and tries to follow each account, respecting the configured filters. All logic runs on the local server — no data is sent to external services other than Instagram itself.
overview.p2: For each account in the list, the app checks the friendship status, applies active filters, and if everything passes, executes the follow. Between each operation, there are deliberate pauses to simulate human behavior and reduce detection risk.

delays.title: Delays between requests
delays.body: After each friendship status check, the app waits between 5 and 15 seconds. After each successful follow, the wait increases to 60 to 120 seconds. These random intervals avoid regular patterns that detection systems easily identify.

batch.title: Batch pauses
batch.body: Every 10 consecutive follows, the app takes an extra break of 2 to 5 minutes. This reproduces the behavior of someone stopping to check the feed, reply to messages, or simply rest.

rateLimit.title: Sliding window rate limit
rateLimit.body: Hourly and daily limits are enforced with a real sliding window — not a simple counter that resets at midnight. If the hourly limit is reached, the app waits until the oldest follow exits the 60-minute window. The day's history is saved in the browser and loaded in the next session, preventing closing and reopening the app from resetting the counter.

retry.title: Retry with backoff on API errors
retry.body: For critical calls (target profile lookup, follower listing), the app retries on HTTP 429 or 503 errors, waiting 30s, 60s, and 90s between attempts. For filter checks, the strategy is to fail fast — if the API doesn't respond, the account is let through instead of freezing the process.

modes.title: Follow modes and risk
modes.intro: Modes control how many follows the app executes per hour and per day. Higher volumes increase the likelihood of triggering Instagram's defense systems.

ultraSafe.label: Ultra Safe
ultraSafe.body: Very low volume, well below Instagram's internal limits. Suitable for new accounts or those that have already experienced restrictions. With current delays, this limit will rarely be reached — the time between operations already keeps the pace low.

safe.label: Safe
safe.body: Balance between speed and safety. Accounts with more than 6 months of regular use generally tolerate this volume without issues. This is the recommended mode for continuous daily use.

risky.label: Risky
risky.body: Volume close to Instagram's internal limits. May trigger temporary action blocks, reach reduction (shadowban), or in repeated cases, account suspension. Use only on established accounts and occasionally, never as a daily routine.

consequences.title: What can happen if limits are exceeded

actionBlocked.label: Action Blocked
actionBlocked.body: Instagram temporarily blocks the follow action, usually for 24 to 48 hours. The account continues to work normally for other actions. It's the most common warning and generally passes without consequences if behavior is corrected.

shadowban.label: Shadowban
shadowban.body: The account's content stops appearing in hashtags and the Explore tab without notice. The profile continues to exist and post normally, but organic reach drops dramatically. It can last days or weeks.

suspension.label: Temporary or permanent suspension
suspension.body: In cases of abusive and repeated use, Instagram may suspend the account temporarily or permanently. This is the most serious situation and rarely happens from isolated follow automation, but the risk exists especially for new accounts or those with a history of violations.

bestPractices.title: Best practices
bestPractices.p1: Use Safe mode as the default. Increase only in specific situations.
bestPractices.p2: Do not run the app 24 hours a day. Simulate human behavior with periods of use and inactivity.
bestPractices.p3: If you receive an "Action Blocked", stop the app immediately and wait at least 24 hours before resuming.
bestPractices.p4: Prefer target accounts with followers in the same niche as your profile — safer and engagement tends to be higher.
bestPractices.p5: The minimum followers filter uses extra API calls. Use sparingly or set a reasonable value for your niche.
bestPractices.p6: The Session ID expires when you log out of Instagram in the browser. Generate a new one whenever the app reports an authentication error.
